steps.title: Solve the unknown directly
steps.content: For no solution, the $x$-coefficients must match while the constants differ. Matching the $x$-coefficients gives $r=\frac{2}{3}$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Start with $$-\frac{4}{21}rx+\frac{s}{6}=12-\frac{8}{63}x.$$ Match the $x$-coefficients: $$-\frac{4}{21}r=-\frac{8}{63}.$$ Multiply both sides by $-1$ and solve: $$\frac{4}{21}r=\frac{8}{63}$$ $$r=\frac{8}{63}\cdot\frac{21}{4}=\frac{168}{252}=\frac{2}{3}.$$

steps.title: Why this gives no solution
steps.content: With $r=\frac{2}{3}$, the $x$-terms are identical on both sides. If the constants are different, the equation reduces to a contradiction like $\frac{s}{6}=12$. The question only asks for the $r$ that makes no solution possible, so the answer is $\frac{2}{3}$.
